pageDescription: Knot picker of translucent pale green jade with brown mottled areas polished to a very high gloss.
extractedText:
artefact | British Museum
Knot picker of translucent pale green jade with brown mottled areas polished to a very high gloss.
Object Type: artefact
Museum number: 2022,3034.164
Description: Knot picker of translucent pale green jade with brown mottled areas polished to a very high gloss.
Cultures/periods: Han dynasty
Production date: 2ndC BC-2ndC AD
Production place: Made in: ChinaAsia: China
Materials: jade
Technique: polished
Dimensions: Length: 11 centimetres
Curator's comments: This bi-shaped knot picker has two triangular extensions, another hooked extension and a long curved and tapering "tail" extension terminating in a sharp point. Eastern Zhou. See Rawson 1995, p.289, cat.no.20:5.
Bibliographic references: Rawson 1995 / Chinese Jade from the Neolithic to the Qing (cat.no.20.5)
Location: On display (Room 33b - Chinese jade, Display Case 12)
Exhibition history: Exhibited:2002, BM, 'Chinese Jade'Previous loan number: 2014,AsiaLoan.27
Previous owner: Previous owner/ex-collection: Sir Joseph Hotung
Acquisition date: 2022
Department: Asia
Registration number: 2022,3034.164
Additional IDs: Miscellaneous number: 214 (Hotung database number)
Production placeMade in: ChinaAsia: China
Made in: ChinaAsia: China
DimensionsLength: 11 centimetresWidth: 5.30 centimetres
Length: 11 centimetres
Width: 5.30 centimetres
